title: trisetum rufescens
intro: Trisetum rufescens, also known as red barley or ruddy oat-grass, is a species of grass native to the Mediterranean region, commonly found in dry areas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and expectorant effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Trisetum rufescens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Trisetum rufescens can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
